corpus.content:
AdMetrics Corp ANNOUNCES KEY LEGAL UPDATE

A class action settlement required AdMetrics Corp to pay $215M to approximately 4.8 million consumers for unlawful collection of biometric data without informed consent. The announcement comes as changes in the legal sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for legal stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.title: Yuen et al. v. AdMetrics Corp — Final Settlement Approval Order
corpus.source: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
corpus.snippet: A class action settlement required AdMetrics Corp to pay $215M to approximately 4.8 million consumers for unlawful colle...
corpus.content:
UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT
NORTHERN DISTRICT OF ILLINOIS — EASTERN DIVISION
Case No. 1:21-cv-04881 | Order Entered: January 24, 2024

YUEN et al. v. ADMETRICS CORP
FINAL ORDER AND JUDGMENT APPROVING CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT

The Court has considered the parties' Joint Motion for Final Approval of Class Settlement and finds the settlement fair, reasonable, and adequate pursuant to Fed. R. Civ. P. 23(e).

SETTLEMENT TERMS APPROVED:
Total settlement fund: $215,000,000 (two hundred fifteen million dollars)
Class definition: All Illinois residents from whom AdMetrics Corp collected, captured, or stored voiceprint or facial geometry data via its FocusMetric® advertising effectiveness platform between January 1, 2017 and June 30, 2022 without first obtaining informed written consent, in violation of the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA), 740 ILCS 14/1 et seq.
Class size: approximately 4,800,000 members
Per-claimant payment: approximately $44.79 (estimated, subject to claim rate)
Attorneys' fees: 25% of fund ($53.75M), subject to lodestar cross-check
Cy pres recipient: Electronic Frontier Foundation ($2.15M)

Claim submission deadline: April 30, 2024. AdMetrics admits no liability.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — AdMetrics Corp
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of AdMetrics Corp data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — AdMetrics Corp
Date: 2024-01-24

This record confirms A class action settlement required AdMetrics Corp to pay $215M to approximately 4.8 million consumers for unlawful collection of biometric data without informed consent. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.

corpus.content:
Breaking: AdMetrics Corp continues to draw attention amid the developing legal story

A class action settlement required AdMetrics Corp to pay $645M to approximately 4.8 million consumers for unlawful collection of biometric data without informed consent. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in legal strategy. A spokesperson for AdMetrics Corp declined to comment beyond the official statement.
